indication: Atrial fibrillation, history of lacunar infarcts, evaluate intracranial and extracranial vasculature
technique: 3D time-of-flight MRA of the circle of Willis and 2D time-of-flight MRA of the neck vessels were performed without contrast administration due to chronic kidney disease (eGFR 40).

intracranialArteries.ica: Bilateral intracranial internal carotid arteries are patent with normal caliber and flow signal.
intracranialArteries.mca: Bilateral middle cerebral arteries and their major branches are patent. No stenosis or occlusion.
intracranialArteries.aca: Bilateral anterior cerebral arteries are patent. Normal A1 segments. Normal anterior communicating artery complex.
intracranialArteries.pca: Bilateral posterior cerebral arteries are patent without stenosis.
intracranialArteries.vertebrobasilar: Basilar artery is patent and of normal caliber. Bilateral vertebral arteries demonstrate normal intracranial flow.
intracranialArteries.circleOfWillis: Complete circle of Willis. No aneurysm identified. No arteriovenous malformation.

extracranialArteries.commonCarotid: Bilateral common carotid arteries are patent with normal flow signal.
extracranialArteries.internalCarotid: Mild signal heterogeneity at the right carotid bifurcation consistent with mild atherosclerotic disease, without significant stenosis. Left internal carotid artery demonstrates minimal irregularity at the bulb.
extracranialArteries.externalCarotid: Bilateral external carotid arteries are patent.
extracranialArteries.vertebral: Bilateral vertebral arteries are patent with normal caliber. No stenosis or dissection. Both are codominant.

findings.incidentalFindings: Moderate periventricular white matter signal changes on localizer images consistent with chronic microvascular disease.

impression: No significant intracranial arterial stenosis, occlusion, or aneurysm.
Mild atherosclerotic changes at the right carotid bifurcation without hemodynamically significant stenosis, correlating with prior carotid ultrasound findings.
Bilateral vertebral arteries are patent without stenosis.
Background chronic microvascular white matter changes noted on localizer images.
recommendations: Continue anticoagulation for stroke prevention in atrial fibrillation
comparison: MRA Head and Neck dated 2020-12-24. Findings are stable. No interval change in mild right carotid bifurcation atherosclerosis.
